meaning: n.花花公子adj.服装华丽的,极好的,上等的
examples: Change places and handy dandy, which is the justice, which is the thief?
That was three days ago when all seemed well and dandy with the world.
FORREST: I'm nothing at all, thank you. My legs are just fine and dandy.
By the end of the century, however, the dandy was emerging as something more.
At the age of twenty-one, in 1906, Modigliani arrived in Paris as a vivacious dandy.
